Research_Statement_FreeText: I'm fascinated by how neural circuits form and change during development, especially the synaptic mechanisms that go wrong in neurodevelopmental disorders. I've spent the last semester in a cell culture and confocal microscopy rotation, running PCR and Western blots on mouse cortical neuron samples. I'm hoping to join a basic-science neuroscience lab where I can keep building these wet-lab skills toward a PhD in neurobiology.

Hours_Available: Available less than 10 hours per week
Research_Statement_FreeText: I love turning messy genomic datasets into a clear biological story - most of my coursework has been in Python and R, and I recently used DESeq2 to run differential expression analysis and BLAST for sequence alignment on a public RNA-seq dataset profiling tumor versus normal tissue. I'm interested in genome analysis and machine learning approaches to variant calling and gene expression, and I'd love to bring those skills to a computational genomics lab as I think about a career in biotech.

Experience_Level: Two or more years of lab experience
Research_Statement_FreeText: I want to understand how tumors evade the immune system, which is what pulled me toward translational cancer research after graduation. I've run flow cytometry, ELISA, and CRISPR-Cas9 knockouts in a cancer immunology lab for the past two years, and I'm now looking for a lab connected to clinical cancer research as I apply to MD/PhD programs.

Experience_Level: Two or more years of lab experience
Research_Statement_FreeText: I'm interested in how a protein's 3D structure determines whether a drug can bind it, which is why I've spent the last two years learning HPLC-based protein purification and using AlphaFold2 and PyMOL to model candidate drug targets. I'd like to join a structural biology lab where I can connect computational structure prediction with hands-on protein work, on my way to a PhD in basic science.

Hours_Available: Available less than 10 hours per week
Research_Statement_FreeText: I care about health disparities and how research can actually change public health policy, not just describe a problem. I've done statistical analysis in R - including logistic regression on survey data - and helped manage a REDCap database for a systematic literature review on chronic disease outcomes during a summer internship, and I'm hoping to find a public health research lab connected to government or population health work.

I'm drawn to the intersection of computation and neuroscience - specifically how machine learning models can make sense of large-scale neural recordings instead of analyzing one neuron at a time. I've built Python and MATLAB pipelines to process EEG and calcium-imaging data for a computational neuroscience course project. I'm looking for a lab where computational modeling and real neural data come together, and I'm ultimately aiming for a PhD in computational neuroscience.

I am interested in understanding how genetic variants contribute to complex disease. I have experience with RNA-seq data analysis in Python using DESeq2 and STAR. I want to pursue a PhD in computational genomics.

My research interest is in how the innate immune system detects and responds to viral pathogens. I have worked with primary human PBMCs and am proficient in flow cytometry and ELISA. I'm aiming for a PhD in basic immunology, with an eventual focus on how innate immune signaling could be leveraged for antiviral therapies.

Experience_Level: Two or more years of lab experience
Research_Statement_FreeText: I am passionate about cancer immunotherapy and specifically want to understand why some patients respond to checkpoint inhibitors while others do not. I have experience with flow cytometry and ELISA and am comfortable working with patient-derived samples. My goal is to pursue a PhD in tumor immunology and contribute to the next generation of immunotherapy research.

Hours_Available: Available less than 10 hours per week
Research_Statement_FreeText: I want to use computational approaches to understand how single cells make decisions during development - specifically how transcriptional programs drive lineage commitment. I am learning Python and R independently and have completed an online course in RNA-seq analysis, including differential expression and pseudotime trajectory methods. I'm excited by the intersection of biology and data science and hope to eventually pursue a PhD in computational biology focused on developmental genomics.

I am interested in health disparities research and how social determinants affect chronic disease outcomes in marginalised communities. I have experience with REDCap and SPSS and want to pursue a career in public health research or government agencies like the CDC.

As a postdoctoral researcher, I study how small-molecule inhibitors bind their protein targets to disrupt aberrant signaling in cancer cells, using enzyme kinetics and substrate binding assays to characterize new drug candidates. I have several years of hands-on experience with HPLC-based compound purification and ELISA-based binding assays, and I'm now looking to apply these skills in a translational pharmacology lab focused on drug discovery. My long-term goal is to move into an industry role in pharmaceutical R&D.

Experience_Level: One academic year of lab experience
Research_Statement_FreeText: I'm interested in designing wearable biosensors that can continuously monitor biomarkers like glucose or cortisol using electrochemical detection methods. I've used MATLAB to process and filter noisy physiological signal data as part of my Master's coursework, and I'm building my Python skills for analyzing sensor calibration datasets. I'd like to join a biomedical engineering lab developing diagnostic devices, with the eventual goal of starting a medical device company.

Hours_Available: Available less than 10 hours per week
Research_Statement_FreeText: After ten years working in environmental consulting, I've decided to go back to school to study how environmental exposures - air pollution, water contamination, extreme heat - drive chronic disease in vulnerable communities. I'm building my statistical analysis skills in R and want to learn formal epidemiological methods like exposure modeling and cohort study design. My goal is to move into environmental health research at a state or federal public health agency.

I'm interested in how genomic biomarkers can help predict which patients will respond to a given cancer treatment before they start it, bridging basic science and the clinic. I have laboratory experience running ELISA assays and some exposure to RNA-seq data as part of a translational oncology project back home. As an international student from Japan, I'm hoping to build US-based clinical research experience toward a career in translational medicine, ideally through an MD/PhD program.

As a first-generation college student, I didn't grow up knowing research was a career path, but a microbiology class on antibiotic resistance changed that for me. I'm especially interested in how viruses evolve resistance to antiviral drugs, and I've done basic PCR and mammalian cell culture work in my coursework labs. I'm looking for a paid research position where I can learn virology techniques hands-on and work toward a PhD in basic science.
